communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Florida financial fusion center system
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

- The last ten bulletins released by the Financial Crime Analysis Center through the Florida Fusion Center; and
- The column headers and database dictionary for the Financial Online Request Tracking System, or any documents sufficient to detail the columns, tables or fields in the FORTS database.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: Confirmation of Public Records Request
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting the Florida Department of Law Enforcement Office of Open Government. This message is being provided as confirmation your message was received. You should receive a response to your inquiry as soon as possible.



If you need criminal history records, please visit our website, https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/Criminal-History-Records/Obtaining-Criminal-History-Information.aspx, and/or contact FDLE’s Criminal History Services Section at ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us> or (850) 410-8161.



Public records requests are processed in the order that they are received. Processing time may be lengthened due to the nature and extent of the request. Searches may be negative and FDLE may not have records responsive to your public records request. You may also receive an invoice for your request depending on the nature and extent of your public records request. Please feel free to contact us at (850) 410-7676, or publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us>, if you have additional specific information relating to the records you are requesting, as this may help expedite your request and/or reduce costs that may be associated with the request.



Additional information regarding how to obtain public records including what costs may be applicable is contained from the hyperlink below. We are committed to our fundamental values of Service, Integrity, Respect, and Quality.



For Additional Information click here: Open Government (state.fl.us)<https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/Open-Government>

title: George W. Bush Presidential Library:  Divestiture of Columbian Chemicals Company by Phelps Dodge Corporation (2005–2007)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: George W. Bush Presidential Library:  Divestiture of Columbian Chemicals Company by Phelps Dodge Corporation (2005–2007)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552) and, to the extent applicable, the Presidential Records Act (44 U.S.C. §§ 2201–2209), I hereby request access to and copies of all records — including policy memoranda, email correspondence, inter-agency briefing papers, and economic reports — created or received by the Executive Office of the President (EOP) between January 1, 2005, and January 1, 2008, regarding the sale and divestiture of Columbian Chemicals Company by Phelps Dodge Corporation.

Scope of Request:

I am specifically seeking records from the National Economic Council (NEC), the National Security Council (NSC), and the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) that discuss:

The March 2006 transaction wherein Phelps Dodge sold its wholly owned specialty chemicals subsidiary, Columbian Chemicals Company, to a joint venture owned by One Equity Partners (JPMorgan Chase) and DC Chemical Co. Ltd. (South Korea).

Any inter-agency or White House communications analyzing the vulnerability of U.S. rubber, defense, and automotive supply chains resulting from the transfer of major carbon black manufacturing assets to foreign or private equity control.

Any records detailing the CFIUS (Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States) review process or national security clearance protocols triggered by South Korean-based DC Chemical Co. Ltd.'s acquisition of an ownership stake in domestic U.S. chemical infrastructure — specifically including the Marietta, Georgia headquarters and the Calumet, Louisiana processing facility (St. Mary Parish).

Correspondence or meeting minutes involving representatives of JPMorgan Chase / One Equity Partners and White House economic advisors regarding the financialization and restructuring of the domestic specialty chemicals sector during this period.

Statutory Acknowledgment regarding Section 721(c):

I recognize that Section 721(c) of the Defense Production Act (50 U.S.C. § 4565) provides a statutory exemption for information filed with CFIUS. However, this request specifically excludes the raw "notice" or "declaration" filed by the private corporate parties. Instead, it seeks the derivative executive branch records — such as policy memoranda, NSC/NEC briefing slides, trade impact studies, and inter-agency correspondence — that discuss the broader national security and macroeconomic implications of the carbon black manufacturing industry and the structural shift toward private equity ownership of critical infrastructure. I request that all reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions of these records be released pursuant to 5 U.S.C. § 552(b).

Search Keywords:
To facilitate an exhaustive search of the Electronic Records Archive (ERA), please utilize the following keywords:
"Columbian Chemicals", "Phelps Dodge", "One Equity Partners", "OEP", "JPMorgan Chase", "DC Chemical", "OCI Company", "Carbon Black", "CFIUS", "Specialty Chemicals Divestiture", "Marietta", "Calumet", "St. Mary Parish"

If a search results in the determination that certain parts of this request or certain portions must be redacted please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed.

I am studying the Kremlin's efforts to use social media to meddle in American politics and I believe that the ownership of Columbian Chemicals was an economic factor that drove an early attempts by the Kremlin to meddle in American culture on social media. This request is in the public interest. Thank you for everything the Bush library does and I look forward to working with you on the resolution of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Pitt County, North Carolina: Flock Safety (Greenville Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to Flock Safety (https://www.flocksafety.com) license-plate-reader hardware and/or software. Documents being requested here should include - but are not limited to - the following:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents of any Flock Safety product/service
- Locations of all installed and planned installation of Flock Safety products within your jurisdiction
- Brochures or product/service specifications regarding Flock Safety
- Privacy and/or retention policies regarding Flock Safety product/services
- Any/all manuals regarding Flock Safety product/services
- Guidelines, bulletins, and briefings mentioning Flock Safety product/services
- Any/all digitally disseminated publications from your agency mentioning Flock Safety product/services

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Nehemiah Williams

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Marjane Satrapi
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into Iranian-French author Marjane Satrapi (22 November 1969 – 4 June 2026). Her name was spelled مرجان ساتراپی in Persian. Her death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find her New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/04/world/europe/marjane-satrapi-persepolis-death.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an investigative journalist who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, Reason Magazine, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: DO NOT USE Network Audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I would like this search (7ffcfe86-4145-4e56-b1f5-5ab1f7b0daad) provided to me without the column level redactions:

ID	 Name	 Org Name	 Total Networks Searched	 Time Frame	 License Plate	 Reason	 Case #	 Filters	 Search Time

7ffcfe86-4145-4e56-b1f5-5ab1f7b0daad	***	DO NOT USE 	5911	"02/12/2026, 12:00:35 AM UTC 03/14/2026, 12:00:35 AM UTC"			***	***	03/13/2026, 11:33:56 PM UTC


I have provided the full search information originally provided to me so it will hopefully be easier for you to find.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jason Hunyar
